channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: GOP-led states are emboldened to keep rolling back trans rights. Democrats struggle with a response
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Republicans and Democrats in Kansas agree that concerns about the economy drove voters to support President Donald Trump by a 16% margin. They also know that ads from Trump and others targeting transgender rights resonated with voters.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, JOHN HANNA and NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump finds new ways to flex presidential power after returning to White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is swiftly breaching the traditional boundaries of presidential power as he returns to the White House, bringing to bear a lifetime of bending the limits in courthouses, boardrooms and politics to forge an expansive view of his authority.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump defends pardons for rioters and suggests Proud Boys could have place in politics
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on his first full day in office Tuesday defended his decision to grant clemency to people convicted of assaulting police officers during the 2021 attack on the Capitol and suggested there could be a place in American politics for the Proud Boys and Oath Keepe
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Coast Guard surging assets to Gulf of America, other waterways to support Trump’s executive orders
item.description: In support of President Trump's executive orders, the Coast Guard will send additional resources to maritime borders, the military branch said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s former sister-in-law alleges abuse against second wife in affidavit to Senate
item.description: Senators vetting the nomination of Minnesotan Pete Hegseth for defense secretary received an affidavit Tuesday from a former sister-in-law alleging that the onetime Fox News host was abusive to his second wife, to the point where she feared for her safety. Hegseth denies the allegations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Estimated 24.6 million TV viewers watched inauguration coverage, smallest audience since 2013
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An estimated 24.6 million television viewers watched President Donald Trump's second inauguration, the smallest audience for the quadrennial ceremony since Barack Obama's second inauguration in 2013.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican senators issue warning to historic museums ahead of pro-life march: ‘Respect’ free speech
item.description: Sens. Cruz and Young urge D.C. museums to respect First Amendment rights of pro-life protesters after Smithsonian kicked out students in 2023 for wearing pro-life gear.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Henderson mayor threatens legal action against councilwoman following ‘spurious rumors’ of extramarital affair
item.description: A litany of personal attacks has enveloped the city of Henderson, and elected officials are coming forward to explain why distrust has taken over a part of city hall.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump highlights partnership investing $500 billion in AI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday talked up a joint venture investing up to $500 billion for infrastructure tied to artificial intelligence by a new partnership formed by OpenAI, Oracle and SoftBank.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration directs all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on leave
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is moving to end affirmative action in federal contracting and is directing that all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on paid leave and eventually be laid off.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Olson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: New Ohio and Florida Senate-appointees sworn in as Vance and Rubio’s replacements
item.description: Two new senators were sworn in on Tuesday to replace new Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California sues Trump administration over executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: (FOX40.COM) — California Attorney General Rob Bonta announced his office would be joining 17 other states in suing the federal government over the newly inaugurated President Donald Trump's executive order ending birthright citizenship for those born in the country. The 14th Amendment to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota and other states sue to stop Trump’s order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: Attorneys general from 18 states including Minnesota sued to block President Donald Trump’s move to end a decades-old immigration policy known as birthright citizenship guaranteeing that U.S.-born children are citizens regardless of their parents’ status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Immigration top priority for Trump and Florida lawmakers
item.description: Solving the illegal immigration crisis is a priority for both President Donald Trump and Florida Governor Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk already has a White House email address
item.description: "It seems to confirm he's going to have an EEOB office," the source said, using the acronym for Eisenhower Executive Office Building.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: News site focused on Elon Musk to launch
item.description: Musk News, a new twice-weekly news site, will focus on the billionaire and Trump advisor's influence and actions over the course of the new presidency.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Group behind failed Florida recreational marijuana amendment trying again for 2026
item.description: The group behind the 2024 ballot measure that would have enshrined recreational marijuana access in the Florida Constitution is giving it another shot in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC
